assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met -- (1) single license filter, (2) multi-license filter, (3) invalid license 400 response, (4) pagination integration, (5) unchanged response shape |'. Each criterion is individually detailed in criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md, all marked PASS with code-level evidence.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in the diff match the task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created) |'. The Domain Findings section includes a file-by-file table: list.rs -> Files to Modify (Match), service/mod.rs -> Files to Modify (Match), tests/api/package.rs -> Files to Create (Match). Line 37: 'No files outside the task specification were touched.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR |'. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate |'. No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). All content is purely analytical: reading code, verifying criteria, and reporting results. No output file mentions modifying, editing, or writing to any code file. The report footer confirms it is an AI-generated comment (line 125: 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr').

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents detailed findings across all domains. No mention of merging, approving, or auto-merging the PR appears anywhere in the outputs. The report is structured as an informational comment ('This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr') for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, or credentials found in added lines |'. The Security domain findings (lines 56-68) include a detailed scan table showing 7 pattern categories (passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, env files, cloud credentials, database credentials) all with 'No' in the Found column.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/package.rs` is a new file with 80 lines; no existing tests were modified or removed |'. The Style/Conventions section (lines 120-122) elaborates: '`tests/api/package.rs` is a newly created file (index `0000000..a1b2c3d`). All 80 lines are additions. No existing test files were modified or had tests removed. This is a purely additive test change.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Domain Findings section has four domain headers: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 25-50) containing Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability; 'Security' (lines 52-68) containing Sensitive Pattern Scan; 'Correctness' (lines 70-92) containing CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands; 'Style/Conventions' (lines 94-122) containing Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, and Test Change Classification. The summary table (lines 3-16) assembles all verdicts including Test Quality (line 13) which combines sub-verdicts: 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no copy-paste patterns detected; tests follow Given/When/Then structure; Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: file-by-file table mapping each diff file to its task section with Match status (lines 31-36), plus per-file line counts (lines 41-44). Security: 7-row pattern scan table with Found/Lines Checked columns (lines 58-66). Correctness: per-criterion code-level verification with inline Rust code snippets and behavioral analysis in criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md, summarized in lines 80-88. Style/Conventions: per-test-function scenario descriptions (lines 99-103), exact doc comment quotes (lines 109-113), Given-When-Then structure observation (line 114), and file-level additive classification with git index reference (lines 120-122).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 117-118: '**Eval Quality -- N/A**' / 'No eval result reviews exist on this PR.' The Test Quality summary row (line 13) shows PASS with 'Eval Quality: N/A', confirming that the N/A Eval Quality does not affect the combined Test Quality verdict (which is PASS based on Repetitive Test Detection and Test Documentation alone).

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 117-118). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. Root-Cause Investigation is N/A with 'No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate' (report.md line 6), confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate |'. These two rows appear in the summary table (lines 3-16) before the Domain Findings section (which begins at line 23), structurally separated from the four domain sub-agent sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions). Neither Review Feedback nor Root-Cause Investigation appears under any domain heading.
